title: Vande Bharat Express Loan Scheme 2026: Financing Options for Railway Contractors and Suppliers
metaDescription: Get financing options for Vande Bharat Express projects with benefits like low interest rates and easy repayment terms
tableOfContents: Introduction to Vande Bharat Express Loan Scheme
Eligibility Criteria for Vande Bharat Express Loan
Application Process for Vande Bharat Express Loan
Important Considerations for Vande Bharat Express Loan
intro: The Vande Bharat Express Loan Scheme 2026 offers financing options to railway contractors and suppliers, enabling them to participate in the prestigious Vande Bharat Express project. With low interest rates and easy repayment terms, this scheme is an attractive opportunity for businesses looking to grow. In this article, we will delve into the details of the Vande Bharat Express Loan Scheme, including eligibility criteria, application process, and important considerations.

sections.heading: Introduction to Vande Bharat Express Loan Scheme
sections.content:
The Vande Bharat Express Loan Scheme 2026 is a financing program designed to support railway contractors and suppliers in their participation in the Vande Bharat Express project. The scheme is offered by various banks, including State Bank of India, ICICI Bank, and Axis Bank, with loan amounts ranging from Rs. 10 lakhs to Rs. 10 crores. The interest rates for these loans vary between 8% to 12% per annum, with repayment terms of up to 7 years. For instance, the State Bank of India offers a loan of up to Rs. 5 crores at an interest rate of 9.5% per annum, with a repayment term of 5 years. The ICICI Bank, on the other hand, offers a loan of up to Rs. 10 crores at an interest rate of 10.5% per annum, with a repayment term of 7 years.
The Vande Bharat Express project is a flagship initiative of the Indian Railways, aiming to provide high-speed rail services across the country. The project requires a significant amount of investment, and the loan scheme is designed to support businesses in meeting this requirement. The scheme is expected to benefit not only the railway contractors and suppliers but also the overall economy, by creating new job opportunities and stimulating economic growth.
To be eligible for the Vande Bharat Express Loan Scheme, businesses must meet certain criteria, including a minimum turnover of Rs. 1 crore, a minimum credit score of 650, and a minimum experience of 3 years in the railway industry. The loan application process involves submitting a detailed business plan, financial statements, and other required documents to the lending bank.

sections.heading: Comparison of Financing Options
sections.content:
The Vande Bharat Express Loan Scheme offers various financing options to railway contractors and suppliers. The following table compares the financing options offered by different banks:
| Bank | Loan Amount | Interest Rate | Repayment Term |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| State Bank of India | Up to Rs. 5 crores | 9.5% per annum | 5 years |
| ICICI Bank | Up to Rs. 10 crores | 10.5% per annum | 7 years |
| Axis Bank | Up to Rs. 7.5 crores | 10% per annum | 6 years |
The comparison table shows that the ICICI Bank offers the highest loan amount, while the State Bank of India offers the lowest interest rate. The Axis Bank offers a loan amount of up to Rs. 7.5 crores at an interest rate of 10% per annum, with a repayment term of 6 years.
It is essential to carefully evaluate the financing options and choose the one that best suits your business needs. You should consider factors such as loan amount, interest rate, repayment term, and other terms and conditions before making a decision.

sections.heading: Eligibility Criteria for Vande Bharat Express Loan
sections.content:
To be eligible for the Vande Bharat Express Loan Scheme, businesses must meet the following criteria:
1. Minimum turnover: The business must have a minimum turnover of Rs. 1 crore in the last financial year.
2. Minimum credit score: The business must have a minimum credit score of 650.
3. Minimum experience: The business must have a minimum experience of 3 years in the railway industry.
4. Registration: The business must be registered with the Ministry of Corporate Affairs or the State Government.
5. Tax compliance: The business must be tax compliant and have filed its income tax returns for the last 3 years.
The eligibility criteria are designed to ensure that only serious and credible businesses participate in the Vande Bharat Express project. The lending banks may also have additional eligibility criteria, and it is essential to check with them before applying for the loan.

sections.heading: Step-by-Step: How to Apply or Get Started
sections.content:
To apply for the Vande Bharat Express Loan Scheme, follow these steps:
1. Visit the website of the lending bank and download the loan application form.
2. Fill in the application form with the required details, including business information, financial statements, and other documents.
3. Submit the application form along with the required documents to the lending bank.
4. The lending bank will review the application and verify the documents.
5. If the application is approved, the lending bank will disburse the loan amount to the business.
The application process typically takes 7-10 working days, and the lending bank may charge a processing fee of up to 1% of the loan amount.
You can also apply for the loan through the online portal of the lending bank or through a loan aggregator website. For more information, you can visit the website of the Indian Railways or the website of the lending bank.

sections.heading: Important Things to Know Before You Decide
sections.content:
Before applying for the Vande Bharat Express Loan Scheme, it is essential to consider the following factors:
1. Interest rates: The interest rates for the loan scheme vary between 8% to 12% per annum, and you should carefully evaluate the interest rates before making a decision.
2. Repayment terms: The repayment terms for the loan scheme vary between 5 to 7 years, and you should ensure that you can repay the loan amount within the specified term.
3. Processing fees: The lending bank may charge a processing fee of up to 1% of the loan amount, and you should factor this into your cost calculations.
4. Collateral: The lending bank may require collateral to secure the loan, and you should ensure that you have the necessary assets to pledge as collateral.
5. Credit score: The lending bank will evaluate your credit score before approving the loan, and you should ensure that you have a good credit score to increase your chances of approval.
It is also essential to read the loan agreement carefully and understand the terms and conditions before signing it.

faqs.q: What is the minimum loan amount that can be availed under the Vande Bharat Express Loan Scheme?
faqs.a: The minimum loan amount that can be availed under the Vande Bharat Express Loan Scheme is Rs. 10 lakhs.

faqs.q: What is the maximum loan amount that can be availed under the Vande Bharat Express Loan Scheme?
faqs.a: The maximum loan amount that can be availed under the Vande Bharat Express Loan Scheme is Rs. 10 crores.

faqs.q: What are the eligibility criteria for the Vande Bharat Express Loan Scheme?
faqs.a: The eligibility criteria for the Vande Bharat Express Loan Scheme include a minimum turnover of Rs. 1 crore, a minimum credit score of 650, and a minimum experience of 3 years in the railway industry.

faqs.q: Which banks offer the Vande Bharat Express Loan Scheme?
faqs.a: The Vande Bharat Express Loan Scheme is offered by various banks, including State Bank of India, ICICI Bank, and Axis Bank.
